scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}}, was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}}, and the breach occurred at {{PLACE_OF_BREACH}}; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

2. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

3. The suit is within limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell whereby the Defendant agreed to sell and the Plaintiff agreed to purchase the residential flat bearing No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement, including the sale price, property description, and timeline for execution, are recorded in the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024, marked as Annexure A.

5. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement to Sell, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 itself via RTGS. The transaction is evidenced by the bank receipt bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654, marked as Annexure B. The remaining balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution and registration of the Sale Deed.

6. The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract. The Plaintiff has arranged the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- and kept the funds available for payment since the date of the agreement. The Plaintiff remained prepared to pay the balance amount and complete the transaction within the stipulated timeline.

7. As per the agreement, the possession of the suit property was to be handed over to the Plaintiff upon execution of the Sale Deed and payment of the full consideration. The Plaintiff has not taken possession of the property as the Sale Deed has not yet been executed due to the Defendant's refusal.

8. The Plaintiff continued to maintain her readiness and willingness to perform the contract throughout the period leading up to the date fixed for execution. The Plaintiff kept the balance funds ready and awaited the Defendant's cooperation to finalize the transaction as per the agreed terms.

PARTICULARS OF BREACH

9. On 05.06.2024, prior to the date fixed for execution, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the Sale Deed. In the said letter, the Defendant claimed that he had received a higher offer for the property and thereby expressed his inability to honor the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024 is marked as Annexure C.

10. Upon receipt of the Defendant's refusal, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed as per the agreement and to honor his contractual obligations. The legal notice was sent via post and is evidenced by the postal receipt and acknowledgment card, marked as Annexure D.

11. The Defendant failed to comply with the legal notice and expressly refused to execute the Sale Deed despite the Plaintiff's demand for performance. The Defendant did not offer to return the advance amount nor did he propose any alternative arrangement to fulfill the contract.

12. Consequently, the Sale Deed could not be executed on the agreed date of 10.07.2024. The Defendant's failure to appear for registration and execute the conveyance document constitutes a clear breach of the terms of the Agreement to Sell, preventing the completion of the transaction.

LOSS AND DAMAGE

13. Due to the Defendant's breach and refusal to execute the Sale Deed, the Plaintiff has been deprived of the use of the advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- paid to the Defendant since 10.01.2024. The Plaintiff has suffered financial loss on account of the blocking of these funds without any return or benefit from the property.

14. The Plaintiff has incurred expenses towards the issuance of the legal notice and other incidental costs in attempting to secure the performance of the contract. These costs were necessitated solely due to the Defendant's wrongful refusal to honor the agreement.

15. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation for the delay and loss of opportunity caused by the Defendant's breach. The Plaintiff claims interest on the advance amount and compensation for the costs incurred, without claiming compound interest as the contract does not expressly provide for the same.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Sections 10 and 14 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

17. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform his/her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action first arose on 10.07.2024 when the Defendant failed to execute the Sale Deed as fixed in the agreement, and subsequently refused to perform upon legal notice.

19. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for specific performance subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the sale deed in respect of the suit property in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration of Rs. {{BALANCE_CONSIDERATION}}/-;
    (b) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff (if not already in possession);
    (c) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
    (d) Award pendente lite and future interest on the amount paid under Section 34 CPC respectively;
    (e) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
    (f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper;

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

Annexure D — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ___

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A company registered under the Companies Act, 2013,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru.
Represented by its Director, Priya Menon.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A Limited Liability Partnership registered under the LLP Act, 2008,
Having its registered office at: Whitefield, Bengaluru.
Represented by its Designated Partner, Vikram Singh.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER SECTION 73 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The suit is valued above the specified value as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

2. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the agreement was entered into in Bengaluru, was to be performed in Bengaluru, and the breach occurred within the local limits of this Court; in any event, the Defendant carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

4. The suit is within limitation under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

5. The Plaintiff states that it has complied with the mandatory pre-institution mediation requirement under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. [Insert: Details of mediation conducted including Authority, Date, and Outcome OR State that urgent interim relief is sought exempting mediation].

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a written Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system. The total contract value was fixed at Rs. 45,00,000/-. A copy of the said Agreement is annexed hereto as Annexure A.

7. Pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid the first milestone payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant on 01.02.2024 via NEFT. The transaction bears UTR Number ICIC20240201456789. Proof of payment is annexed hereto as Annexure B.

8. Under the express terms of the Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system on or before 01.05.2024. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module of the ERP system by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024.

9. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without completing the deliverables. Email correspondence evidencing the abandonment is annexed hereto as Annexure C.

10. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant dated 01.07.2024 demanding performance and refund. A copy of the legal notice is annexed hereto as Annexure E.

11. The Defendant has failed to respond to the legal notice and has not refunded the advance amount paid by the Plaintiff.

LOSS AND DAMAGE

12. The Plaintiff is entitled to the refund of the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid to the Defendant.

13. Due to the non-delivery of the module and abandonment of the project, the Plaintiff suffered consequential losses amounting to Rs. 12,00,000/- arising from delay in operations. The loss assessment report by the auditor is annexed hereto as Annexure D.

14. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the claimed amount from the date of breach until realization.

15. The Plaintiff took steps to mitigate the loss by issuing the legal notice and commissioning an audit report to quantify the damages suffered.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

17. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to sue for damages to accrue.

19. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for damages subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to the ad valorem rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. 32,00,000/- as damages for breach of contract;
    (b) Award pendente lite interest at the rate of 18% per annum from the date of breach / institution of suit till decree under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
    (c) Award future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit from the date of decree till realisation under Section 34 CPC;
    (d) Award costs of the suit including advocate's fees to the Plaintiff;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper;

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Actual and direct financial loss (Refund of Advance)
     Amount claimed: Rs. 20,00,000/-
     Basis / methodology: Breach of Contract / Unjust Enrichment
     Documentary proof: Annexure B (NEFT Receipt)

(ii) Consequential Damages (Operational Loss)
     Amount claimed: Rs. 12,00,000/-
     Basis / methodology: Auditor's Loss Assessment Report
     Documentary proof: Annexure D

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. 32,00,000/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024

Annexure B — NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789)

Annexure C — Email correspondence showing abandonment

Annexure D — Loss assessment report by auditor

Annexure E — Legal notice dated 01.07.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at Bengaluru on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, Priya Menon, the Director of the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 20 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

Priya Menon

PLAINTIFF

STATEMENT OF TRUTH

I, Priya Menon, the authorised representative of the Plaintiff herein, do hereby state on solemn affirmation that the contents of the above plaint and the facts stated in paragraphs 1 to 20 are true to my knowledge and belief and not
